englishDefinitions: Used other than figuratively or idiomatically: see go, over.
(transitive, idiomatic) To look at carefully; to scrutinize; to analyze.
(intransitive, idiomatic) To create a response or impression.
(graffiti) To spray paint graffiti over someone else's graffiti.
(transitive, slang, dated, UK) To pat down (someone) and then rob them.
(intransitive, slang, dated) To turn out successful; to be well-received.
(intransitive, dated) To convert to Roman Catholicism.
(intransitive, by extension) To convert or switch sides.
persianTranslation: به غیر از معنای مجازی یا اصطلاحی استفاده می شود: رجوع کنید به رفتن، روی.
(متعدی، اصطلاحی) با دقت نگاه کردن. موشکافی کردن؛ برای تجزیه و تحلیل
(ناگذر، اصطلاحی) برای ایجاد پاسخ یا برداشت.
(متعدی، عامیانه، تاریخ، انگلستان) دست زدن به (کسی) و سپس سرقت از آنها.
(غیر گذرا، عامیانه، تاریخ دار) To turn out موفق; مورد استقبال قرار گیرد.
(غیر گذرا، مورخ) برای گرویدن به کاتولیک رومی.
(غیر گذرا، با پسوند) برای تبدیل یا تغییر طرف.
examples: I'm going over to my friend's house.
Please go over the reports to make sure we haven't missed anything.
Playing a radio in the office did not go over well with his coworkers.
In first period, we're going over the basics of cell biology.
That's who went over the butcher.
That song really went over with the audience.
He went over to the finance department's side in this dispute.
